request.question: Does Empire of the Sun or 3 Doors Down have more band members?

title: Empire of the Sun (band)
Bigger Than Life Tour
3 Doors Down (album)
Citizen/Soldier
Away from the Sun
3 Doors Down
Here Without You
When I'm Gone (3 Doors Down song)
The Doors discography
Seventeen Days
sentences: Empire of the Sun is an Australian electronic music duo from Sydney, formed in 2007.
The band is a collaboration between Luke Steele of alternative rock act The Sleepy Jackson, and Nick Littlemore of electronic dance outfit Pnau.
Their 2008 debut album "Walking on a Dream" brought the duo international success and has been certified double platinum in Australia and gold in the United Kingdom.

The album provided a number of internationally charting singles including "Walking on a Dream" which peaked at number ten on the Australian ARIA Singles Chart and reached number sixty four on the UK Singles Chart, and "We Are the People", which peaked at number fourteen on the UK Singles Chart.
The Bigger Than Life Tour was a co- headlining tour by American rock bands, Daughtry and 3 Doors Down.
The tour supported Daughtry's third studio album, "Break the Spell", and 3 Doors Down's "The Greatest Hits" album.
3 Doors Down is the self-titled fourth studio album by American rock band 3 Doors Down, released on May 20, 2008. "
It's Not My Time" was the first single from the album and was released in February 2008.
It can be heard on the band's MySpace site.
Another song on the album, "Citizen/Soldier" was released in 2007 as a tribute to the National Guard.
"Citizen/Soldier" is a single by the American alternative rock band 3 Doors Down from their self-titled album "3 Doors Down".
The song was released as a single in November 2007 in conjunction with a recruitment campaign by the United States National Guard.
The lyrics convey the band's views regarding the actions performed by the Guard.
It was released as the third single for American active rock and mainstream rock radio stations in November 2008, while it is their fourth overall single.
Away from the Sun is the second studio album by American rock band 3 Doors Down, released on November 12, 2002.
The singles released for the album were as follows: "When I'm Gone", "The Road I'm On", "Here Without You" and "Away from the Sun".
The recording sessions took place during the summer of 2002 with Producer/Engineer Rick Parashar at London Bridge Studios, in Seattle, Washington.
Rush guitarist Alex Lifeson performed live with the band during their premiere party in Biloxi for "Away from the Sun".
The album has sold four million copies worldwide, including well over three million in the US alone.
3 Doors Down is an American rock band from Escatawpa, Mississippi, that formed in 1996.
The band originally consisted of Brad Arnold (lead vocals/drums), Todd Harrell (bass), and Matt Roberts (lead guitar, backing vocals).
They were soon joined by rhythm guitarist Chris Henderson.
The band rose to international fame with their first single, "Kryptonite", which charted in the top three on the "Billboard" Hot 100 chart.
The band then signed with Republic Records and released their debut album, "The Better Life", in 2000.
The album was the 11th-best-selling album of the year and was certified 6x platinum in the United States.
They were later joined by drummer Richard Liles, who played during the tour for their first album.
"Here Without You" is a song by American rock band 3 Doors Down.
It was released in August 2003 as the third single from the album "Away from the Sun".
It peaked at No. 5 on the "Billboard" Hot 100, for the week ending November 8, 2003.
Only their songs "Kryptonite" and "When I'm Gone" reached higher positions on the chart, peaking at No. 3 and No. 4, respectively.
It has since been certified 2x Platinum in the United States and Platinum in Australia.
The song was a large success on pop radio, becoming their third No. 1 hit on the Mainstream Top 40 chart and their first No. 1 on the Adult Top 40 chart, where it stayed at the top for 13 weeks.
"When I'm Gone" is a song by American rock band 3 Doors Down.
It was released in October 2002 as the lead single from their second album, "Away from the Sun".
It peaked at  4 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 chart in April 2003.
The song also spent seventeen weeks atop the "Billboard" Hot Mainstream Rock Tracks chart from November 2002 through March 2003, becoming one of the longest-running number-one singles on that chart.
The song also peaked  2 on the Hot Modern Rock Tracks chart almost reaching  1 by one spot from Queens of the Stone Age's "No One Knows" on the issue dated February 15, 2003.
It spent one week atop the Top 40 Mainstream chart for one week on May 3, 2003 and also peaked at  3 on the Adult Top 40 chart.
The following is the discography of the American rock band The Doors.
Formed in Los Angeles in 1965, the group consisted of Jim Morrison (vocals), Ray Manzarek (keyboards), John Densmore (drums), and Robby Krieger (guitar).
The Doors became one of the most popular rock bands of their era.
Their debut album, "The Doors" (1967), released by Elektra Records, charted at No. 2 on the US "Billboard" 200 and produced the group's most successful single, "Light My Fire".

The album received several sales certifications including a 4 times multi-platinum from both the Recording Industry Association of America (RIAA), and from the Canadian Recording Industry Association (CRIA).

The Doors' second studio album, "Strange Days" (1967), often recognized as their most creative output, failed to produce a hit single as popular as "Light My Fire", though the album sold well commercially but did not reach the same level of success as the debut.
It reached No. 3 on the "Billboard" 200 and was certified platinum in the United States and Canada.
The Doors' third studio album "Waiting for the Sun" (1968), was regarded as an artistic disappointment when compared to their earlier material.
However, commercially it was very successful and reached No. 1 in the US and France, and produced their second No. 1 single, "Hello, I Love You".
"Waiting for the Sun" was the first Doors album to chart in the United Kingdom, where it peaked inside the Top 20.
The album was certified gold in that country by the British Phonographic Industry (BPI), as well as being certified gold and platinum in several other countries.
Seventeen Days is the third studio album by American rock band 3 Doors Down, released on February 8, 2005, five years to the day that 3 Doors Down released their debut album, "The Better Life".

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Task: Compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a single concise, retrieval-oriented intermediate state to guide the next-hop retrieval. Do not produce the final answer or perform any new retrieval here.

Required content of `summary` (single field):
- A short header identifying the primary topic(s) found in the passages (1 line).
- A concise enumeration of the concrete facts supported verbatim by the provided passages: list entity mentions and directly supported factual spans (dates, years, titles, roles). Use very short quoted fragments (<= ~15 words) only when those words appear verbatim in the passages. Keep to facts present in the passages; do not add or infer new facts.
- Explicitly expose any ambiguity or missing information that blocks answering: if multiple candidate entities or page variants appear, enumerate those candidates and label the name as ambiguous; if a required target fact is missing or ambiguous, label it as the missing fact and identify the bridge entity that connects this hop to the next.
- Prioritized next-hop retrieval directives (1–4 items): concrete page titles or disambiguation pages to fetch, explicit article sections to inspect (e.g., lede/infobox, "Career", "Publication", "History"), or tight search keywords. Order them by priority and be specific (include possible disambiguation terms or section names).

Formatting and style constraints:
- Keep the entire `summary` concise: a short header plus 1–4 brief sentences or 2–6 short bullet lines in total.
- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.
- If a name or term has multiple distinct variants in the passages, do not pick one without disambiguating evidence; enumerate candidates instead.
- Do not invent or add facts not present in the supplied passages. Avoid long verbatim quotations; prefer short extracts (<= ~15 words) or paraphrase.

Output expectations: produce exactly one field `summary` containing the concise, retrieval-oriented summary that meets the above requirements.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions (must follow exactly):
- Emit a single field named `summary` containing only 1–3 concise sentences that integrate the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into a grounded post-retrieval state.
- If the `context` already contains a concise answer sentence, keep that sentence as the first sentence of `summary` unless the `passages` clearly contradict it; append supporting sentence(s) after it rather than replacing it.
- Preserve the primary answer name(s) exactly as they appear in the inputs (context or passages) unless those names clearly conflict with stronger passage content.
- When one or more `passages` supply short biographical, membership, partnership, or corroborating facts about a named entity from the `context`, append at most one brief supporting sentence per relevant named person. Each such supporting sentence should:
  - begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the passages (if available),
  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

response.hop1_query: Does Empire of the Sun or 3 Doors Down have more band members?
hop1_docs: Another 700 Miles | Another 700 Miles is a live EP by 3 Doors Down released on November 11, 2003.  It was recorded live at a performance in Chicago, Illinois during the band's Away from the Sun tour.

High Tyde | High Tyde are an English indie pop quartet from Brighton, England.  The ages of the band members range from 18 to 19.  They have played at major music festivals, such as Boardmasters, Dot 2 Dot, Underground, Fieldview, Reading, and Y Not.  They have also played support shows for Little Comets, Bad Suns, Young Kato, and Peace.  They have been featured on BBC Radio 1.  The sound of the band is inspired from indie bands like Two Door Cinema Club and Foals.  Their sound also has similarities of bands like The 1975 and Bombay Bicycle Club.  Their music gives off a summer vibe.

Away from the Sun | Away from the Sun is the second studio album by American rock band 3 Doors Down, released on November 12, 2002.  The singles released for the album were as follows: "When I'm Gone", "The Road I'm On", "Here Without You" and "Away from the Sun".  The recording sessions took place during the summer of 2002 with Producer/Engineer Rick Parashar at London Bridge Studios, in Seattle, Washington.  Rush guitarist Alex Lifeson performed live with the band during their premiere party in Biloxi for "Away from the Sun".  The album has sold four million copies worldwide, including well over three million in the US alone.

3 Doors Down (album) | 3 Doors Down is the self-titled fourth studio album by American rock band 3 Doors Down, released on May 20, 2008. " It's Not My Time" was the first single from the album and was released in February 2008.  It can be heard on the band's MySpace site.  Another song on the album, "Citizen/Soldier" was released in 2007 as a tribute to the National Guard.

The Road I'm On | "The Road I'm On" is a song recorded by American rock band 3 Doors Down.  It was released in April 2003 as the second single from their second album, "Away from the Sun".  The song reached number 8 on the U.S. "Billboard" Mainstream Rock charts and number 24 on the U.S. "Billboard" Modern Rock charts.

Supralateral arc | A supralateral arc is a comparatively rare member of the halo family which in its complete form appears as a large, faintly rainbow-colored band in a wide arc above the sun and appearing to encircle it, at about twice the distance as the familiar 22° halo.  In reality, however, the supralateral arc does not form a circle and never reaches below the sun.  When present, the supralateral arc touches the (much more common) circumzenithal arc from below.  As in all colored halos, the arc has its red side directed towards the sun, its blue part away from it.

Walking on a Dream (song) | "Walking on a Dream" is a song by Australian electronic music duo Empire of the Sun, which was released on 30 August 2008 as the lead single ahead of their debut album of the same name (3 October 2008).  The track was co-written by band members, Luke Steele and Nick Littlemore with Jonathan "Donnie" Sloan.  The full Sam La More remix was made available as a free download on the band's official website.
response.summary_1:
Header: Band membership evidence for Empire of the Sun and 3 Doors Down

- Empire of the Sun: passages state "Australian electronic music duo Empire of the Sun" and list "band members, Luke Steele and Nick Littlemore".
- 3 Doors Down: passages refer to "American rock band 3 Doors Down" (albums and songs) but do not state a member count or list members.
- Missing / ambiguous: "number of band members in 3 Doors Down" is not provided (bridge entity: 3 Doors Down (band)); also unclear whether the question asks current lineup or original lineup—this must be specified if needed.
- Next-hop retrieval (priority order):
  1. Fetch "3 Doors Down" Wikipedia page — inspect lede/infobox and sections "Members", "Band members", "Current members" and "Past members".
  2. If discrepancy, fetch "3 Doors Down (disambiguation)" and/or news sources for lineup changes; search keywords: "3 Doors Down members list", "3 Doors Down current lineup".
  3. (Confirming Empire of the Sun if needed) Fetch "Empire of the Sun" page lede/infobox or "Members" section to confirm duo and member names.
response.hop2_query: 3 Doors Down members current lineup list
hop2_docs: List of Danzig band members | The list of Danzig band members consists of the original lineup of Glenn Danzig (vocals), John Christ (guitar), Eerie Von (bass) and Chuck Biscuits (drums), spanned seven years and four albums.  The lineup first changed in 1994, when Biscuits was fired from the band due to royalty disagreements.  After he was rejected upon asking to return, and, according to a special issue of "Kerrang!  magazine", former Nirvana drummer Dave Grohl turned down an approach from them, he was replaced by Joey Castillo, who made his first public appearance as a member of Danzig at an in-store signing the day of the fourth album's release.  After promoting the fourth album, John Christ and Eerie Von both left the band in July 1995, leaving Glenn Danzig as the only remaining original member of the band.  Many lineup changes happened since; guitarists included Dave Kushner, Jeff Chambers, Todd Youth, Joe Fraulob and Kenny Hickey, bassists included Josh Lazie, Rob "Blasko" Nicholson, Howie Pyro and Jerry Montano and drummers included Bevan Davies and Karl Rosqvist.  The current Danzig line-up consists of Glenn Danzig on vocals, Tommy Victor on guitar, Steve Zing on bass and Joey Castillo on drums.

List of Transsylvania Phoenix band members | The following is a list of musicians who have been members of Romanian rock and roll band Transsylvania Phoenix since their formation in Timișoara in 1962.  The current lineup consists of founder Nicu Covaci and five other members.

List of Bad Company band members | The following list details musicians who have been and still are members of the American band Bad Company since its formation in 1973.  The band's current lineup includes original members Simon Kirke (drums), Mick Ralphs (guitar) and Paul Rodgers (lead vocals) joined by latter members Howard Leese (guitar, formerly of Heart) and Todd Ronning (bass guitar).  The band has gone through many lineup changes throughout its career.  The only member to appear in every lineup is drummer Simon Kirke.  He is the only member of the band to have performed at every Bad Company concert and to appear on every album.  Mick Ralphs is currently still a part of the band but is being temporarily replaced by former Black Crowes guitarist Rich Robinson for the bands 2016 United States tour.  While still officially a member of the band in 1990-1991, Ralphs also sat out most of their Holy Water tour.  This time he was temporarily replaced by Geoff Whitehorn.

Down by Law (band) | Down by Law is a punk rock band formed in 1989 by former All frontman Dave Smalley, who is the only original member.  The band has recorded 7 studio albums.  They stayed on Epitaph Records until 1998 and have since switched to other labels.  Down by Law has never achieved substantial commercial success, but a number of largely popular acts cite them as influential, and they have an underground following.  After several lineup changes, the band went on hiatus from touring and recording in 2003, but resurfaced in 2008 before beginning work on new material and embarking on their first tour in six years in 2009.  In 2017 the band reunited their acclaimed punkrockacademyfightsong lineup and are currently active and touring.

List of Fozzy band members | The following is a list of musicians who have been members of American heavy metal band Fozzy since their formation in San Antonio, Texas in 1999.  The current lineup consists of three of the original members of the band: vocalist Chris Jericho, guitarist Rich Ward, and drummer Frank Fontsere.

List of Avengers members | The Avengers are a series of fictional superhero teams that have starred in "The Avengers" and related comic book series published by Marvel Comics.  Over the years, the teams have featured a rotating lineup composed of a large number of characters.  Characters listed in bold are the current members of the team.

Dodge Shadow | The Dodge Shadow and Plymouth Sundance are economical 3-door and 5-door hatchbacks that were introduced for the 1987 model year.  For 1991, a 2-door convertible variant was added to the Shadow lineup; but not the Sundance lineup.  The 3-door hatchback model replaced the Dodge Charger (L-body) model, while the 5-door hatchback model replaced the Dodge Omni model; of their respective marque.  With the 1987 acquisition of American Motors by Chrysler from Renault, the Shadow/Sundance also replaced the American Motors-built Renault Alliance sedan and convertible, marking the official withdrawal of Renault from the United States and Canadian markets.
